weakest_point: Vortex gpu_cov is no longer blocked on wrapper existence, zero observability, or warm-cache runtime survival. The fixed-test sgemm Tier-2 packet now runs under A/B/C with nonzero GPU-side real_toggle_subset words, and direct bench reuse cuts the monitored B command from 109.56s to 2.49s with the same compact output. A second standard workload, saxpy, reproduces the same 10/18 packet and the same compact SHA, so the next question is not test-name stability but whether the remaining partial memory-path regions are already sufficient for provisional rule freeze.
next_step: Treat Vortex as the first completed Tier-2 medium-design gate and carry the now-frozen provisional rule packet into late-family validation. Because saxpy does not widen the active region set beyond sgemm, any further medium-strengthening work should target region-budget refinement or a different medium design rather than another current standard Vortex test.
